replacement_target: later explicit portable adapter-contract checkpoint or replacement artifact only

current_program_context.current_product_priority: repo-local playable baseline and repo stabilization
current_program_context.series_role: portable contract extraction preparation that must not compete with playable-baseline work

portable_adapter_definition.summary: A portable adapter is a canonical declarative contract for a bounded translation or mediation path between one source interface or contract and one target interface or contract, including the adapter's identity, purpose, declared capability requirements and exclusions, linked task or evidence or policy surfaces when applicable, compatibility and stability constraints, explicit refusal and incompatibility semantics, and portability limits, without embedding runtime adapter discovery, runtime selection, execution orchestration, transport ownership, or product-shell command dispatch.
portable_now_requires: optional task, evidence/review, and policy linkage
explicit portability, version, and stability markers
portable_adapter_definition.canonical_adapter_contract: Authoritative declaration of a bounded mediation path, including source and target contract identity, constraints, and refusal semantics.
portable_adapter_definition.adapter_implementation: Concrete code that realizes the contract, such as format loaders, pack verifier adapters, render adapters, or projection adapters.
portable_adapter_definition.wrapper_shim_transitional_surface: A local packaging layer, deprecation shim, or compatibility facade that may expose an adapter-shaped interface without defining portable adapter law.
portable_adapter_definition.product_shell_command_surface: Launcher, setup, AppShell, or tool command surfaces that may consume or expose adapter logic but remain shell-owned and local to Dominium.
portable_adapter_definition.runtime_service_boundary: Negotiated attach, transport, loopback, or other runtime boundaries that prove useful refusal and compatibility classes but are not portable adapter contracts now.
portable_adapter_families: projection or transformation declaration shape only where source and target contracts are explicit
dominium_retained_adapter_families: setup and launcher bridge payload surfaces

adapter_id.summary: Stable canonical identifier for the adapter contract.

adapter_kind.summary: Declared category of mediation the adapter performs.

source_interface.summary: Authoritative source interface or source contract consumed by the adapter.

target_interface.summary: Authoritative target interface or target contract produced or served by the adapter.

adapter_purpose.summary: Bounded statement of what the adapter translates, mediates, or normalizes.

capability_requirements.summary: Capability classes that must be present for the adapter path to be valid.

capability_exclusions.summary: Capability classes, modes, or conditions that make the adapter path invalid or unsupported.

task_linkage.summary: Optional linkage to portable task kinds or task identifiers.

evidence_review_linkage.summary: Optional linkage to portable evidence or review requirements.

policy_permission_linkage.summary: Optional linkage to policy or permission shapes that govern lawful use.

compatibility_and_stability.summary: Supported ranges, incompatibility boundaries, and stability markers for the adapter path.

failure_refusal_semantics.summary: Declared unsupported, incompatible, blocked, refused, degraded, ambiguous, and review-gated classes.

fallback_posture.summary: Optional declaration of any lawful degraded or fallback path.

portability_limits.summary: Explicit statement of what parts of the adapter are portable and what remains host-specific or runtime-owned.

version_notes.summary: Schema version, compatibility version, migration notes, or other version markers.

required_boundary_distinctions.summary: The contract defines the mediation path; the implementation is the concrete code that realizes it.
A wrapper packages or exposes an implementation for a local consumer surface without becoming canonical adapter law.
A shim preserves a legacy entrypoint or deprecation path and must remain transitional rather than portable by default.
A contract declares a valid mediation path; a runtime resolver would choose, load, or activate one.
Capability profiles describe capability posture; adapter compatibility decides whether a source-to-target path is valid.
Adapter existence does not imply lawful use, approval, or review clearance.
CLI payloads, dashboards, reports, and summaries may reflect adapter meaning but may not redefine it.

contract_linkage: adapters may declare task_linkage when a task requires a specific mediation path
task contracts may reference adapter contracts when source-to-target translation is mandatory
deferred_runtime_work: runtime selection between competing adapters for the same task
planner behavior based on adapter availability

contract_linkage: adapters may declare evidence emitted, consumed, or required
adapters may declare whether degraded or sensitive paths require review
deferred_runtime_work: review workflow engines tied to adapter execution

contract_linkage: adapters may reference policy and permission identities that govern lawful use
adapters may declare refusal, denial, escalation, or review posture without becoming the policy engine
runtime_linkage_not_frozen: live trust publication or revocation plumbing
deferred_runtime_work: distributed permission resolution for adapter use

contract_linkage: adapters may declare capability requirements and exclusions
capability profiles may establish whether a candidate environment is compatible with the adapter contract
deferred_runtime_work: adapter resolution against live capability environments

repo_grounded_evidence.why_it_matters: Shows real contract mediation with migration, validation, and explicit refusal classes.
Shows a real AppShell adapter that links verification, trust policy, and deterministic output writing.
Shows a thin wrapper that must remain distinguishable from portable adapter law.
Shows stable wrapper rows and adapter-shaped metadata for governed offline tool surfaces.
Shows stable adapter output obligations for local subprocess wrappers without proving runtime portability.
Shows explicit deprecation shims and replacement targets, proving shim and adapter are not interchangeable.
Shows negotiated attach, compatibility mode handling, and explicit refusal when negotiation or acceptance fails.
Shows runtime transport ownership and endpoint descriptors that stay Dominium-retained.
Shows a real model-to-model adapter that is product-specific rather than portable now.
Shows deterministic view adapters tied to Dominium-specific projection layers.
Shows shell bridge payload translation and refusal mapping that remain local to setup surfaces.
Shows adapter inventories with active and coverage adapters, proving that adapter naming alone does not imply portability or completeness.
Shows explicit deterministic incompatibility reason codes.
Shows blocked or refused attach behavior when negotiation is missing.
Shows explicit refusal on skipped IPC negotiation.

portable_now: canonical declaration that an adapter mediates one named source interface or contract into one named target interface or contract
optional task, evidence/review, and policy/permission linkage
explicit compatibility, refusal, incompatibility, blocked, degraded, fallback, and ambiguity posture
explicit portability limits and version notes
the rule that adapter existence does not imply runtime availability, lawful use, or implementation completeness
dominium_retained_now: AppShell command surfaces and subprocess tool adapters under tools/tool_surface_adapter.py
tool wrapper output payload execution semantics under schemas/tool_adapter_output.schema.json
appshell/pack_verifier_adapter.py execution flow and output writing
negotiated IPC and attach flows in appshell/ipc/ipc_client.py and appshell/ipc/ipc_transport.py
render and view adapters in client/render/render_model_adapter.py and geo/projection/view_adapters.py
setup bridge payload translation in tools/setup/setup_cli.py
validation adapter inventories and coverage adapters in validation/validation_engine.py
deferred_until_post_baseline: runtime capability-resolution engines for adapter choice
queueing, scheduling, or worker routing tied to adapter execution
bakeoff or compiler systems for adapter comparison or generation
cross-repo adapter marketplaces or transport-agnostic adapter distribution

failure_refusal_incompatibility_fallback.unsupported_adapter_path: No lawful adapter path exists for the requested source and target combination.
failure_refusal_incompatibility_fallback.incompatible_adapter_path: A candidate adapter exists, but capability, source, target, or version constraints do not match.
failure_refusal_incompatibility_fallback.blocked_adapter_path: The adapter is known, but a required precondition such as negotiation, descriptor presence, or source artifact availability is missing.
failure_refusal_incompatibility_fallback.refused_adapter_path: The adapter path is rejected by declared policy, permission, trust, intent, or review posture even though the path is otherwise known.
failure_refusal_incompatibility_fallback.degraded_or_fallback_adapter_path: A lower-fidelity or restricted path is allowed only when the contract declares it explicitly.
failure_refusal_incompatibility_fallback.ambiguous_adapter_selection: More than one candidate path exists and no canonical contract rule selects among them.
failure_refusal_incompatibility_fallback.review_gated_adapter_use: The adapter path exists but requires review, acknowledgement, approval, or escalation before lawful use.

canonical_truth_includes: declared task, evidence/review, and policy linkage
compatibility, refusal, fallback, and portability limits
derived_views_must_not: redefine the source or target contract
claim portability because a local wrapper exists
reinterpret a shim, bridge, or transport surface as portable adapter law

prohibited_adapter_contract_shapes: embedding runtime adapter engine semantics into the portable contract
treating every wrapper as a portable adapter
treating every shim or legacy bridge as a portable adapter
assuming portability from file location or naming
collapsing capability, permission, and adapter shape into one concept
treating product-shell command rows as canonical adapter truth
treating runtime transport, attach, or session flows as portable adapter contracts by default
using derived payload schemas as proof of extraction readiness
using X-6 to justify immediate broad adapter implementation or repo-wide renaming
downstream_prompts_enabled: later AIDE extraction review over which concrete adapter implementations are portable candidates versus Dominium-retained execution surfaces
later mapping work that links portable tasks to portable adapters without inventing runtime selection machinery
